title: Complexity accounting: hidden K-multiply cost, one-time factorizations, RHS

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Gap G5 (Lemma L3 & L5).** The complexity analysis must be exact and must beat the direct $O(n^3r^3)$.

Points to pin down:
1. $\mathrm{cost}(K\text{-multiply})$ is generically $O(n^2)$; the advertised per-iteration $O(qr+nr^2)$ silently assumes a structured/NystrĂ¶m $K$. State the assumption or use $O(qnr+n^2r)$.
2. One-time costs: Cholesky of $K$ ($O(n^3)$) for the preconditioner, factorization of the $r\times r$ Gram ($O(r^3)$). These must be counted and shown $\ll O(n^3r^3)$.
3. RHS $(I_r\otimes K)\operatorname{vec}(B)=\operatorname{vec}(KB)$: $B=TZ$ is the precomputed MTTKRP (given, or the sparse MTTKRP on the $q$ nonzeros at $O(qr)$), so the subproblem RHS is $O(n^2r)$ and avoids $O(M),O(N)$ â€” this must be stated explicitly.

**Required:** a per-iteration cost, total cost $=\#\text{iter}\times$ per-iter $+$ one-time, and an explicit comparison to $O(n^3r^3)$.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
**Resolved Gap G5 (complexity accounting).** I added three lemmas (structured mat--vec L3, preconditioner+RHS L4, exact complexity L5), two algorithm boxes (direct + PCG), and remarks that address each of the three points, all numerically verified.

**1. Honest K-multiply / per-iteration cost.** Lemma "Structured action of $\mathcal{A}$" proves $\mathcal{A}\,\mathrm{vec}(Y)=\mathrm{vec}(\tilde K^T D\tilde Z+\lambda KY)$ with $D=\mathrm{diag}(c)$, $c_j=\tilde K_{j,:}Y\tilde Z_{j,:}^T$, using only $\tilde K\in\mathbb{R}^{q\times n}$, $\tilde Z\in\mathbb{R}^{q\times r}$, $K$, $Y$ â€” no $N$- or $M$-vector. Exact cost $\mathrm{cost}(\mathcal{A}\text{-mult})=O(qnr+n^2r)$. Remark "Honest mat--vec cost" states explicitly that the dense $K$-multiply $Y\mapsto KY$ is counted at $\Theta(n^2r)$ (NOT assumed structured/NystrĂ¶m); a rank-$\rho$ kernel is noted as an *optional* improvement replacing $n^2r$ by $n\rho r$. Per-iteration cost is $C_{iter}=O(qnr+n^2r+nr^2)=O(qnr)$ (since $q>n,r$), with the $n^2r$ term kept visible. So the previously-advertised $O(qr+nr^2)$ is corrected to the honest $O(qnr+n^2r)$.

**2. One-time costs counted and shown $\ll n^3r^3$.** Preconditioner $P=(Z^TZ)\otimes K^2+\lambda(I_r\otimes K)$ (= $\mathcal{A}$ in the fully-observed limit). Setup: eig of $K$ = $O(n^3)$; the $r\times r$ Gram $G=Z^TZ$ via the Khatri--Rao identity $G=\circledast_{i\neq k}(A_i^TA_i)$ (no order-$M$!), cost $O(r^2\sum_{i\neq k}n_i+dr^2)$; eig of $G$ = $O(r^3)$. Total $C_{setup}=O(n^3+r^3+qdr)$, each piece $\ll n^3r^3$. $P^{-1}$ applies in $O(n^2r+nr^2)$ via simultaneous diagonalization $P^{-1}z=\mathrm{vec}(U[(U^TZ_0V)\oslash\Omega]V^T)$.

**3. RHS without $O(M),O(N)$.** Lemma "Right-hand side" proves $b=(I_r\otimes K)\mathrm{vec}(B)=\mathrm{vec}(KB)$ with the sparse MTTKRP $B=TZ=\sum_j t_j e_{a_j}\tilde Z_{j,:}$ at $O(qr)$ (free if $B$ given), then $KB$ at $O(n^2r)$; total $O(qr+n^2r)$, no $O(M)$/$O(N)$ term â€” stated explicitly.

**Total + comparison.** $C_{PCG}=m\cdot C_{iter}+C_{setup}=O(m\,qnr+n^3+r^3+qdr)$. Ratio to direct: $C_{PCG}/(n^3r^3)=O(mq/(n^2r^2)+1/r^3+1/n^3+qd/(n^3r^2))$. PCG beats the direct $O(n^3r^3)$ iff $m\,q=o(n^2r^2)$; worst case $m\le\mathrm{rank}(\mathcal{A})\le nr$ gives $O(qn^2r^2)$, an improvement by the factor $q/(nr)$ (i.e. when $q=o(nr)$). I state the sufficient condition explicitly rather than asserting $q\ll nr$ unconditionally, since $q<N$ alone does not force it.

**Numerical verification (run_python):** (a) structured mat--vec matches the dense operator to $10^{-13}$; (b) the sparse $q$-nonzero version (using $\tilde K,\tilde Z$) matches to $10^{-14}$; (c) the Khatri--Rao Gram identity holds to $10^{-13}$; (d) sparse MTTKRP matches $TZ$ to machine eps; (e) $P^{-1}$ via simultaneous diagonalization matches the dense solve to $10^{-16}$; (f) a full PCG run converges to relative residual $10^{-13}$.

Written to solution.tex (Lemmas L3, L4, L5 + Algorithms 1--2). Closing as resolved.
